questions.question: Which of the following is the correct order for increasing complexity?
questions.explanation: The biological hierarchy of organization, from simplest to most complex, is: cells → tissues → organs → organ systems → organism.

questions.question: Which of the following statements about the characteristics of life is FALSE?
questions.explanation: Photosynthesis is a process used by plants and some bacteria to obtain energy, but it is not a universal characteristic of life. Animals, fungi, and many other organisms do not perform photosynthesis.

questions.question: Which of the following is regarded as a single cell organism?
questions.explanation: Amoeba is a unicellular organism, meaning it consists of a single cell that carries out all life processes. Mosquito, mushroom, and mango tree are all multicellular organisms composed of many specialized cells.

questions.question: Which of the following statements about experimental design is FALSE?
options: The independent variable is what the scientist changes.
The dependent variable is what is measured.
Controlled variables are kept the same for all groups.
The control group receives the experimental treatment.
questions.explanation: The control group does NOT receive the experimental treatment. It is used as a baseline to compare against the experimental group that does receive the treatment.

questions.question: The sum total of all the chemical reactions occurring in our body is known as
questions.explanation: Metabolism is the sum total of all chemical reactions occurring in a living organism. It includes both catabolic reactions (breaking down molecules to release energy) and anabolic reactions (building up complex molecules from simpler ones).

questions.question: In majority of higher animals and plants, _____ and _____ are mutually exclusive events.
questions.explanation: In most higher animals and plants, growth and reproduction are mutually exclusive events because resources (energy and nutrients) are typically allocated to either growth or reproduction at any given time. For example, many organisms must reach a certain size (maturity) before reproduction begins, and once reproduction starts, growth often slows or stops.

questions.question: Which of the following statements about microscope use is FALSE?
options: Always carry the microscope with two hands.
Start focusing with the lowest power objective.
Use the coarse adjustment knob on high power.
Store the microscope with the lowest power objective in place.
questions.explanation: The coarse adjustment knob should NEVER be used on high power, as it can crash the lens into the slide. Only use the fine adjustment knob on high power.

questions.question:
A living organism, X, can make its own food, get rid of toxic materials and detect and respond to stimuli.

What other four processes must organism X carry out to stay alive?
questions.explanation: The organism already performs: making its own food (nutrition), getting rid of toxic materials (excretion), and detecting/responding to stimuli (sensitivity). The four essential life processes it still needs are: growth, movement, reproduction, and respiration. These, combined with the ones already stated, cover the main characteristics of life.

questions.question: Which of the following statements about data analysis is FALSE?
options: A line graph shows trends over time.
A bar graph compares different categories.
A pie chart shows parts of a whole.
questions.explanation: Qualitative data is descriptive (colors, textures, smells) and does NOT include numbers. Numerical measurements are called quantitative data.

questions.question: Which of the following statements about lab reports is FALSE?
options: The procedure should be detailed enough for someone to replicate the experiment.
The results section includes raw data and observations.
The discussion section explains what the results mean.
The hypothesis is always proven correct in the conclusion.
questions.explanation: A hypothesis is never 'proven' correct. It is either supported or rejected based on the evidence. Science is about testing ideas, not proving them absolutely.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You have a sharp eye for scientific accuracy.
scoreMessages.70: Good job. Review the false statements you missed carefully.
scoreMessages.50: Satisfactory. Focus on distinguishing facts from common misconceptions.
scoreMessages.0: Please review all Unit 1 topics with attention to detail.
